meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debug Smarter: Advanced Analytics Setup for AI Builders
content.seo_description: Stop guessing why your code fails. Learn how to implement advanced analytics with Antigravity and Claude Code to master debugging and ship faster as a student.
content.answer_block: You can't debug what you can't see. By integrating real-time telemetry and structured logging into your stack, you transform silent failures into actionable data. Use Antigravity to instrument your event streams and Claude Code to automate the analysis of these logs, turning obscure crashes into clear, fixable code paths.
content.intro:
The screen flickers. Your terminal spits out a generic 'Error 500,' and the silence in your room is deafening. You are stuck in the purgatory of invisible bugs—a rite of passage for every developer, but a bottleneck that separates amateurs from architects. You don't need more 'print' statements; you need an observability layer that speaks your language.

Today, we move beyond trial-and-error. We are engineering a diagnostic pipeline that pulls back the curtain on your runtime environment. By leveraging Antigravity for telemetry and the reasoning power of Claude Code, you will stop hunting bugs and start surgically removing them, turning your development process into a high-precision operation.

steps.title: Define Telemetry Schemas
steps.description: Establish what data points define a 'healthy' state in your application to catch anomalies before they cascade.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a telemetry schema definition for a SaaS application that tracks user session state, API latency, and critical function execution paths.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create schema.json based on current project architecture'

steps.title: Inject Antigravity Middleware
steps.description: Integrate the instrumentation layer into your core API handlers to capture request-response metadata automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust middleware function using the Antigravity SDK to wrap all incoming requests and log execution time and error payloads.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply 'integrate telemetry middleware into main.py'

steps.title: Automate Error Tracing
steps.description: Configure Claude Code to monitor your logs for specific regex patterns that indicate silent failures.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an automated monitoring script that pulls logs from Antigravity and identifies patterns resembling stack overflow or memory leaks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'scan logs for recurring error patterns'

steps.title: Implement Predictive Debugging
steps.description: Use your analytics data to build a feedback loop where Claude Code suggests refactors based on runtime performance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the performance bottleneck in the provided log data and suggest an optimized algorithm implementation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude fix 'optimize function based on latency logs'

steps.title: Validate with Synthetic Tests
steps.description: Ensure your analytics are firing correctly by simulating failure states and verifying the diagnostic output.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a test suite that simulates high-load failure scenarios to verify that Antigravity accurately captures the event logs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'execute diagnostic test suite and verify log capture'

pro_tips: Always log the 'contextual state'—the inputs that caused the error—not just the stack trace.
Use unique correlation IDs for every user request to trace a single transaction across microservices.
Set up threshold-based alerts for 'silent failures' where the code runs but returns incorrect business logic results.
Periodically prune your logs to keep your storage costs low and your search performance high.
Treat your logs as a 'source of truth' for your product roadmap; build features that users actually use, not what you think they use.
content.next_steps: Now that you have observability, move on to implementing automated CI/CD pipelines that run these diagnostic tests on every pull request.
content.conversion_block: You’ve moved from guessing to knowing. This is the exact workflow that senior engineers use to maintain massive, scalable systems. You have the tools, the strategy, and the mindset to master your own codebase. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
